paper.paper_title: 109 年國中教育會考英語科(閱讀)

questions.question_text: Look at the picture. The man is holding one of the _____.

source_meta.raw_text:
1.   Look at the picture. The man is holding one of the _____.
     (A) bowls
     (B) glasses
     (C) plates
     (D) spoons

questions.explanation: 根據圖片，男子手中拿著的是透明的玻璃杯，故(B) glasses正確。(A) bowls是碗，圖中男子拿的不是碗；(C) plates是盤子，形狀與圖片不符；(D) spoons是湯匙，是小型餐具，與圖片中男子手持的物品大小和形狀都不相符。

questions.question_text:
My mom puts the _____ on to keep her hands away from water when she washes
the dishes.

source_meta.raw_text:
2.   My mom puts the _____ on to keep her hands away from water when she washes
     the dishes.
     (A) gloves        (B) jacket           (C) ring          (D) socks

questions.explanation: 題目描述媽媽洗碗時為了保護雙手不接觸水而穿戴某物，「keep her hands away from water」表示要隔離雙手與水的接觸。(A) gloves（手套）能完全覆蓋雙手並防水，符合此目的，故 (A) 正確。(B) jacket（夾克）是上衣，無法保護手部；(C) ring（戒指）反而會沾水，不符邏輯；(D) socks（襪子）是穿在腳上，與洗碗無關。

questions.question_text:
Can you _____ your feet a little off the floor?  I want to see if my keys are under the
sofa.

source_meta.raw_text:
3.   Can you _____ your feet a little off the floor?  I want to see if my keys are under the
     sofa.
     (A) check          (B) kick                 (C) raise             (D) show

questions.explanation: 題目要求填入適當動詞，使句意完整。根據上文「I want to see if my keys are under the sofa」，說話者需要看沙發下方，因此要求對方「把腳抬起來一點」，故(C) raise正確。raise意為「舉起、抬起」，符合語境。(A) check「檢查」與(B) kick「踢」都不符合邏輯，check無法與feet搭配，kick則與「抬起」的目的相悖；(D) show「展示」也不適當，因為重點是抬起腳而非展示。

questions.question_text:
_____ of my sisters are older than I am.  I’m the youngest of the three children in my
family.

source_meta.raw_text:
4.   _____ of my sisters are older than I am.  I’m the youngest of the three children in my
     family.
     (A) Both             (B) Few               (C) Most             (D) Some

questions.explanation: 題幹說「我是家中三個孩子中最年輕的」，表示有兩個姐姐都比我年長。空格前後為「_____ of my sisters are older than I am」，需填入能修飾「my sisters」（我的姐姐們）的量詞。由於只有兩個姐姐，且兩個都比我年長，故應填 Both（兩個都），表示「我的兩個姐姐都比我年長」，(A) 正確。(B) Few 意為「少數幾個」，與「兩個都」不符；(C) Most 意為「大多數」，用於三個以上時較合適；(D) Some 意為「某些」，過於模糊且無法確定具體數量。

questions.question_text:
The beach near our town is a very _____ place. Every summer at least one kid dies
there in the water.

source_meta.raw_text:
5.   The beach near our town is a very _____ place. Every summer at least one kid dies
     there in the water.
     (A) dangerous       (B) exciting        (C) lonely        (D) popular

questions.explanation: 題幹提到「每年夏天至少有一個小孩在水裡溺水身亡」，這是一個負面且危險的事實，因此空格應填入表示「危險」的形容詞。(A) dangerous 正確，意為「危險的」，與後文溺水死亡的內容相符。(B) exciting 興奮的、(C) lonely 孤獨的都無法解釋為何會有人溺水；(D) popular 受歡迎的雖可能與海灘相關，但無法說明死亡事件的因果關係，故 (A) 正確。

questions.question_text:
My wife enjoys going to the movies with her friends, but I like to watch TV at home
by _____.

source_meta.raw_text:
6.   My wife enjoys going to the movies with her friends, but I like to watch TV at home
     by _____.
     (A) I              (B) me               (C) myself             (D) mine

questions.explanation: 空格前有介詞 by，後面應填反身代詞表示「獨自、單獨」之意，句意為「我喜歡在家獨自看電視」。by myself 是固定搭配，表示「我自己、獨自」，故 (C) 正確。(A) I 為主格代詞，不符介詞後用法；(B) me 為受格代詞，無法表達「獨自」含義；(D) mine 為所有格代詞，語法不符。

questions.question_text:
For the past twenty years, my father _____ in a school library.  But he’ll leave the job
next month.

source_meta.raw_text:
7.   For the past twenty years, my father _____ in a school library.  But he’ll leave the job
     next month.
     (A) worked           (B) has worked      (C) is working         (D) works

questions.explanation: 題幹關鍵在於「For the past twenty years」（過去二十年來）搭配「But he'll leave the job next month」（但他下個月會離職），表示從過去持續到現在的動作即將結束。此時應用現在完成式 has worked，表示「一直在做且持續到現在」，故 (B) 正確。(A) worked 為簡單過去式，無法表達持續性；(C) is working 為現在進行式，不符合「過去二十年」的時間跨度；(D) works 為簡單現在式，同樣無法表達這段期間的持續動作。

questions.question_text:
It’s great that we can buy train tickets over the phone without going out.  It _____ us
a lot of time.

source_meta.raw_text:
8.   It’s great that we can buy train tickets over the phone without going out.  It _____ us
     a lot of time.
     (A) lends             (B) prepares          (C) saves           (D) takes

questions.explanation: 句意為「我們可以透過電話購買火車票而不用出門，這為我們節省了很多時間」。空格處需填入動詞，與受詞 us 和 a lot of time 搭配。save sb. time 是常用片語，表示「為某人節省時間」，故 (C) saves 正確。(A) lend 意為「借出」，不符語境；(B) prepare 意為「準備」，無法與 time 搭配；(D) take 雖可與 time 搭配但表示「花費時間」，與句意相反。

questions.question_text: Mozart _____ his first music when he was only six years old.

source_meta.raw_text:
9.   Mozart _____ his first music when he was only six years old.
     (A) is writing      (B) has written     (C) will write       (D) wrote

questions.explanation: 題幹敘述莫札特六歲時的過去事件，需用過去簡單式。「when he was only six years old」明確指出過去時間點，故應填過去式動詞 wrote，(D) 正確。(A) is writing 為現在進行式，表現在進行中；(B) has written 為現在完成式，強調對現在的影響；(C) will write 為未來式，均不符合過去事件的時間語境。

questions.question_text:
Jill: Have you decided _____ you will celebrate your 30th birthday?
Sue: Yeah, I’m going to have a big barbecue party.

source_meta.raw_text:
10. Jill: Have you decided _____ you will celebrate your 30th birthday?
    Sue: Yeah, I’m going to have a big barbecue party.
    (A) how              (B) where           (C) what              (D) when

questions.explanation: 空格前有 decided，後接疑問詞引導的間接問句。Jill 問的是慶祝方式，Sue 回答「I'm going to have a big barbecue party」說明如何慶祝，故 (A) how 正確。(B) where 詢問地點，但 Sue 未提及地點；(C) what 詢問事物，文法上不符；(D) when 詢問時間，但題幹已明確提及「30th birthday」，不需再問時間。

questions.question_text: Alison doesn’t like _____ what to do.  She only does things she wants to do.

source_meta.raw_text:
11. Alison doesn’t like _____ what to do.  She only does things she wants to do.
    (A) told             (B) to tell          (C) be told         (D) to be told

questions.explanation: 本題考查動詞 like 後接動名詞或不定式的用法。句意為「Alison 不喜歡被告知該做什麼」，需要被動語態表達「被告知」的含義。like 後可接「to be + 過去分詞」的被動不定式結構，故 (D) to be told 正確。(A) told 為過去式，文法不符；(B) to tell 為主動不定式，無法表達「被告知」的被動意義；(C) be told 缺少不定式標記 to，在 like 後不符合英文文法規則。

questions.question_text: Josh has planned to make a trip to New York and _____ some of his friends there.

source_meta.raw_text:
12. Josh has planned to make a trip to New York and _____ some of his friends there.
    (A) visit           (B) visits          (C) visiting       (D) visited

questions.explanation: 句子結構為「has planned to make a trip and _____ some of his friends」，兩個動作由 and 連接，應使用平行結構。前半「to make」是不定詞，後半也應為不定詞「to visit」，省略重複的 to 後為「visit」，故 (A) 正確。(B) visits 為第三人稱單數現在式，破壞平行結構；(C) visiting 為現在分詞，與不定詞結構不符；(D) visited 為過去式，時態與 has planned 不搭配。

questions.question_text:
Ms. Johnson has been taking phone calls since she entered the office this morning.
Just when she thought she could finally leave work, _____ call came in.

source_meta.raw_text:
13. Ms. Johnson has been taking phone calls since she entered the office this morning.  
    Just when she thought she could finally leave work, _____ call came in.
    (A) another         (B) each              (C) the next        (D) the other

questions.explanation: 句子表示Ms. Johnson整個上午一直在接電話，就在她以為終於可以下班時，又有一通電話進來。空格需填入表示「又一通」的詞彙，「another call」意為「另一通電話」，符合「又有電話」的語境，故(A)正確。(B)each強調個別性，不符合「又來一通」的意思；(C)the next通常指「下一個」但需有明確順序參照，此處無此脈絡；(D)the other指「另外那一個」，用於兩者之間的對比，不適用於此開放性情境。

questions.question_text:
We were so sure that Jerry _____ well on the difficult job.  His past experience in
other work showed he was the right guy for it. So when he failed, no one believed it.

source_meta.raw_text:
14. We were so sure that Jerry _____ well on the difficult job.  His past experience in
    other work showed he was the right guy for it. So when he failed, no one believed it.
    (A) had done        (B) did              (C) has done           (D) would do

questions.explanation: 本題考查過去時間點的推測或預期。題幹關鍵在「We were so sure that Jerry _____ well」，「were so sure」表示過去時間點我們的確信或預期，後接 that 子句應用「would do」表示當時對未來的推測。故 (D) 正確。(A) had done 表完成式，不符合「預期他會做好」的語意；(B) did 為簡單過去式，無法表達「當時的推測」；(C) has done 為現在完成式，時態與過去的確信不搭配。

questions.question_text:
For Mike, the price is _____ important thing when he shops for jeans. He cares even
more about the shape and the size of the pockets.

source_meta.raw_text:
15. For Mike, the price is _____ important thing when he shops for jeans. He cares even
    more about the shape and the size of the pockets.
    (A) the more         (B) the most         (C) the less       (D) the least

questions.explanation: 題幹關鍵在於後句「He cares even more about the shape and the size of the pockets」，表示Mike關心口袋的形狀和大小更勝於價格，即價格是他最不在乎的因素。空格應填最高級形容詞，表「最不重要」，故(D) the least正確。(A) the more和(B) the most皆表較重要或最重要，與文意相反；(C) the less為比較級，不符合「最___」的句型結構。

media.passage_text:
When it arrives,
Everyone cheers.
The shirts and shorts dance
On the clothesline,
The trees and flowers wave
Like they’re saying hi,
And the clouds are so excited
To have a running race.
When it comes,
Dad’s face falls.
His coat and jeans jump
Off the clothesline
Into the pond.
The tree leaves hop off
And have a party on his new car.
When it gets here,
A lot of fun things I notice:
The pond’s face,
Sad and old with lines;
Mrs. Smith’s bread,
Delicious and freshly baked.
But what smell is it?
Oh, no, it’s from Mr. Brown’s feet!
 leaf (leaves) 葉子

questions.question_text: What is it in the reading?

source_meta.raw_text:
16. What is it in the reading?
    (A) The sun.          (B) The rain.       (C) The wind.     (D) The rainbow.

questions.explanation: 詩文中「The shirts and shorts dance / On the clothesline, / The trees and flowers wave」、「His coat and jeans jump / Off the clothesline / Into the pond」、「The tree leaves hop off」等描寫衣物飄動、樹葉飛舞的現象，明確指向風的作用，故(C)正確。(A)太陽不會使衣物跳動；(B)雨水會使衣物濕透而非舞動，且詩中未提及濕潤；(D)彩虹無法推動物體移動，與詩意不符。

questions.question_text: How does Dad feel“when it comes”?

source_meta.raw_text:
17. How does Dad feel“when it comes”?
    (A) He is scared.
    (B) He is excited.
    (C) He is not happy.
    (D) He is not interested.

questions.explanation: 根據詩文「When it comes, Dad's face falls. His coat and jeans jump Off the clothesline Into the pond.」可知，當風來臨時，爸爸的臉「falls」（沉下來），他的衣物被吹進池塘，樹葉吹到他的新車上造成損壞，這些都是負面的情況。故(C)正確。(A)害怕過度解讀，詩中未提及恐懼；(B)興奮與文意相反，爸爸的反應明顯是不開心；(D)不感興趣也不符合，爸爸是有明確的負面情緒反應。

media.passage_text:
Hey, guys! I have a lot of things I don’t need, so I’m
thinking about having a yard sale. What do you think?
Having a yard sale is not easy. You have to collect things, put
prices on them, put them out on tables and then wait for people
to come. And usually you have to sell things at VERY low prices.
For all that time and work, you make very little money. Why not
just sell your things online for higher prices?
Debby’s right. But I think it’d be even better to just give your
things to people who need them. I just sent out a lot of things
last week.
Hey, I love yard sales! It’s a great chance to make big money if
you pick the right day. In my experience, Saturday is the best.
I agree with Albert. You can make money and meet interesting
people. I made some good friends at my last yard sale.

questions.question_text: What can we learn about Buffy’s friends?
options: Matt has experience of yard sales.
Jamie had a successful yard sale last week.
Albert thinks weather is important for yard sales.
Debby thinks things are cheaper online than at yard sales.

source_meta.raw_text:
18. What can we learn about Buffy’s friends?
    (A) Matt has experience of yard sales.
    (B) Jamie had a successful yard sale last week.
    (C) Albert thinks weather is important for yard sales.
    (D) Debby thinks things are cheaper online than at yard sales.

questions.explanation: 根據材料，Albert說「It's a great chance to make big money if you pick the right day. In my experience, Saturday is the best」，表明Albert有舉辦yard sale的經驗，故(A)正確。(B)錯誤，Jamie說的是「made some good friends at my last yard sale」，未提及上週；(C)錯誤，Albert強調的是選對日期（Saturday），非天氣；(D)錯誤，Debby建議「sell your things online for higher prices」，意指線上價格更高而非更便宜。

questions.question_text: What do Buffy’s friends think about having a yard sale?
options: Albert and Matt think it’s a nice way to make friends.
Debby and Albert think it’s difficult to make money from it.
Matt agrees with Jamie that it’s a good chance to help people.
Jamie agrees with Debby that it’s a lot of trouble to prepare for it.

source_meta.raw_text:
19. What do Buffy’s friends think about having a yard sale?
    (A) Albert and Matt think it’s a nice way to make friends.
    (B) Debby and Albert think it’s difficult to make money from it.
    (C) Matt agrees with Jamie that it’s a good chance to help people.
    (D) Jamie agrees with Debby that it’s a lot of trouble to prepare for it.

questions.explanation: 根據材料，Debby說「Having a yard sale is not easy. You have to collect things, put prices on them...For all that time and work, you make very little money」，強調準備工作繁重；Jamie則說「I just sent out a lot of things last week」表示寧願捐贈，暗示認同Debby的麻煩之處。故(D)正確。(A)錯誤，Albert提到「make big money」和「meet interesting people」，但Albert是看好yard sale的；(B)錯誤，Debby認為麻煩多但賺錢少，Albert卻認為「pick the right day」能「make big money」，兩人意見相反；(C)錯誤，Jamie主張捐贈而非yard sale，與Albert的看法不同。

media.passage_text:
Here is an ad for Sunny Market.
From Jan. 4 to Feb. 4, for each $50 you spend With Sunny Sun pictures, you
(on anything EXCEPT books and CDs) at Sunny can get one of the prizes below
Market, you’ll get a Sunny Sun picture     .     for free or at low prices!!
(2 pictures for every $100, 3 pictures for every
$150, …)                                   ★         Sunny ★Cup        Chocolate
★ Collect 12 pictures, and you can get a Sunny
Cup for free!
★ Collect 10 pictures, and you can get any of
the desserts on the right for free!    ★          Fruitcake
★           Ice cream
★ Collect 6 pictures, and you can buy a Sunny
Cup for $200, or any of the desserts on the
right for $100!
★       From Jan. 4 to Feb. 10, you can use Sunny
Sun pictures to get Sunny Cups or desserts.

questions.question_text: From the ad, which is true about Sunny Sun pictures?
options: The first day to use Sunny Sun pictures is Feb. 4.
The last day to collect Sunny Sun pictures is Feb. 10.
You cannot use Sunny Sun pictures to get free desserts.
You cannot get Sunny Sun pictures when you buy books.

source_meta.raw_text:
20. From the ad, which is true about Sunny Sun pictures?
    (A) The first day to use Sunny Sun pictures is Feb. 4.
    (B) The last day to collect Sunny Sun pictures is Feb. 10.
    (C) You cannot use Sunny Sun pictures to get free desserts.
    (D) You cannot get Sunny Sun pictures when you buy books.

questions.explanation: 根據廣告開頭「for each $50 you spend (on anything EXCEPT books and CDs) at Sunny Market, you'll get a Sunny Sun picture」，購買書籍和光碟時無法獲得Sunny Sun圖片，故(D)正確。(A)錯誤，因為廣告明確指出「From Jan. 4 to Feb. 4」可以收集圖片，首日是1月4日而非2月4日；(B)錯誤，收集圖片的截止日是2月4日，而使用圖片的截止日才是2月10日；(C)錯誤，廣告清楚表示「Collect 10 pictures, and you can get any of the desserts on the right for free」，可以用圖片免費獲得甜點。

questions.question_text: Sammy has 7 Sunny Sun pictures.  She wants to get a Sunny Cup.  How can she get one?
options: Use 6 Sunny Sun pictures and pay $100.
Use 6 Sunny Sun pictures and pay $200.
Spend $150 to get 3 more Sunny Sun pictures.
Spend $200 to get 4 more Sunny Sun pictures.

source_meta.raw_text:
21. Sammy has 7 Sunny Sun pictures.  She wants to get a Sunny Cup.  How can she get one?
    (A) Use 6 Sunny Sun pictures and pay $100.
    (B) Use 6 Sunny Sun pictures and pay $200.
    (C) Spend $150 to get 3 more Sunny Sun pictures.
    (D) Spend $200 to get 4 more Sunny Sun pictures.

questions.explanation: Sammy 現有 7 張圖片，要取得 Sunny Cup。根據廣告「Collect 12 pictures, and you can get a Sunny Cup for free」，她需要 12 張圖片才能免費獲得；或根據「Collect 6 pictures, and you can buy a Sunny Cup for $200」，她可用 6 張圖片加付 $200 購買。Sammy 有 7 張圖片，超過 6 張的要求，故 (B) 正確。(A) 錯誤因為用 6 張圖片購買 Sunny Cup 需付 $200 而非 $100；(C) 和 (D) 雖能增加圖片數，但不是最直接的方式，且 (C) 花費 $150 只得 3 張（共 10 張仍不足 12 張），(D) 花費 $200 得 4 張（共 11 張仍不足 12 張）。

media.passage_text:
Now I’m going to show you how to work with chocolate. I’ll
do it in a “bain-marie,” or, well, some people call it “water bath.”
I myself love the name “bain-marie.” To make a “bain-marie,”
you need two pots, one bigger than the other.
First, break the chocolate into small pieces and put them in the
smaller pot. Usually when making chocolate desserts, you’ll need to
mix chocolate with butter and sugar. So if you do, put them in the
pot too.
Now, half fill the bigger pot with warm water. Put the smaller
pot over the bigger one, and start to slowly mix the chocolate, the butter, and
the sugar together. Keep the water under 50ºC, or the chocolate may lose its
shine. And make sure that no water goes in the smaller pot, or the chocolate will
become hard and cannot be used.
Keep mixing for about five to ten minutes, and the job is done. Easy, right?
 mix 混合

questions.question_text:
From the reading, which picture best shows the “bain-marie” way of working with
chocolate?

source_meta.raw_text:
22. From the reading, which picture best shows the “bain-marie” way of working with
    chocolate?
    (A)               (B)                (C)                    (D)

questions.question_text: What does them mean in the reading?

source_meta.raw_text:
23. What does them mean in the reading?
    (A) Bains-marie.                          (B) Butter and sugar.
    (C) Chocolate desserts.                   (D) Small pieces of chocolate.

questions.explanation: 根據文章「Usually when making chocolate desserts, you'll need to mix chocolate with butter and sugar. So if you do, put them in the pot too.」，them 指的是前句提到的 butter and sugar，故 (B) 正確。(A) 錯誤因為 bains-marie 在此段落未被提及需放入鍋中；(C) chocolate desserts 是名詞片語，不符合 them 的代詞用法；(D) small pieces of chocolate 已用 them 代稱過，不會再用 them 指代。

questions.question_text: From the reading, which is true about working with chocolate?
options: It is better to use a “bain-marie” than a “water bath.”
The water in the pot should not be warmer than 50ºC.
It is better to finish mixing it in less than five minutes.
We should not mix butter and sugar together at the same time.

source_meta.raw_text:
24. From the reading, which is true about working with chocolate?
    (A) It is better to use a “bain-marie” than a “water bath.”
    (B) The water in the pot should not be warmer than 50ºC.
    (C) It is better to finish mixing it in less than five minutes.
    (D) We should not mix butter and sugar together at the same time.

questions.explanation: 材料明確指出「Keep the water under 50ºC, or the chocolate may lose its shine」，表示水溫不應超過50°C，故(B)正確。(A)錯誤，因為「bain-marie」和「water bath」是同一種方法的不同名稱，材料說「some people call it 'water bath'」，並無優劣之分。(C)錯誤，材料建議「Keep mixing for about five to ten minutes」，表示需要5至10分鐘，不是越快越好。(D)錯誤，材料說「you'll need to mix chocolate with butter and sugar」，表示可以同時混合這三種材料。

media.passage_text:
Taiwanese Bian-dang in Manhattan, New York City
5/30/ 2014    by Eva Schmidt
During lunch time, on 53rd Street, you’ll see
a long line of people in front of a Taiwanese food
truck for their “bian-dang,” a Taiwanese word for
“lunch box.” On the menu you can find fried chicken with rice, pork dumplings,
tea eggs, and other popular Taiwanese dishes.
The owners of the food truck are Thomas and Diana Yang, two
Taiwanese-Americans. This brother-sister team started their business in 2009.
Back then, there were a few Taiwanese restaurants in the city and several thousand
food trucks, but the Yangs were the first to sell Taiwanese food on a food truck.
At first, business was slack. People thought they were just another Chinese food
truck, and the food truck’s name, “Cravings,” often made people think of desserts, not
lunch. Few people would stop by and try their food. “I couldn’t even give away free
food,” said Thomas. To catch people’s eye, they painted their truck blue, and drew
Taiwan’s national flower, the plum blossom, on it. They also changed the food truck’s
name to “bian-dang.” Soon people started to notice them, and business finally got
better. Now on a good day, they can sell about 150 bian-dangs in about two hours.
So what’s next for Thomas and Diana? Well, they hope one day there will be a
block with Taiwanese food in the city, just like there is a Chinatown, a Korea Town,
and a Little Italy. And their bian-dang food truck might just help to make that happen.
 national 國家的

questions.question_text: What does the news story say about the Yangs’ food truck?
options: What people love about their food.
What made them start their business.
How they made delicious fried chicken.
How they fixed their business problems.

source_meta.raw_text:
25. What does the news story say about the Yangs’ food truck?
    (A) What people love about their food.
    (B) What made them start their business.
    (C) How they made delicious fried chicken.
    (D) How they fixed their business problems.

questions.explanation: 文章重點在於楊氏兄妹如何解決生意冷清的問題。材料明確指出「At first, business was slack」，但他們透過「painted their truck blue, and drew Taiwan's national flower, the plum blossom, on it. They also changed the food truck's name to 'bian-dang'」等策略改善，「Soon people started to notice them, and business finally got better」，故(D)正確。(A)只是提及菜單內容，非新聞重點；(B)文中未說明創業原因；(C)文章未討論烹飪方法，只列舉菜色。

questions.question_text: What does it mean when business is slack?

source_meta.raw_text:
26. What does it mean when business is slack?
    (A) It is bad.     (B) It is for sale.  (C) It is growing.       (D) It is open every day.

questions.explanation: 文中提到「At first, business was slack. People thought they were just another Chinese food truck...Few people would stop by and try their food.」，說明當business is slack時，顧客很少光臨，生意不好，故(A) It is bad.正確。(B)是for sale（待售）與文意無關；(C)是growing（成長）與slack的意思相反；(D)是open every day（每天營業）文中未提及。

questions.question_text: What does that mean in the news story?
options: A city block that sells Taiwanese food.
A business that is popular across the country.

source_meta.raw_text:
27. What does that mean in the news story?  
    (A) A Taiwanese restaurant.
    (B) A second Taiwanese food truck.
    (C) A city block that sells Taiwanese food.
    (D) A business that is popular across the country.

questions.explanation: 題目問「that」在新聞故事中指什麼。根據文末段落，「they hope one day there will be a block with Taiwanese food in the city, just like there is a Chinatown, a Korea Town, and a Little Italy」，「that」指的是這個希望實現的願景，即「a block with Taiwanese food in the city」，故(C)正確。(A)混淆了餐廳與街區的概念；(B)文中未提及第二台食品車；(D)的「across the country」超出原文範圍，原文只談紐約市內的一個街區。

media.passage_text:
This is a letter by Selena Bieber to the students in her school.
http://www.nvhsstudsup.org/openletter
Later Is Better!
Selena Bieber
Northville High School
Do you feel it’s hard to get up early for school and even harder to listen
well in the first class at 8 a.m.? Well, this happens to most of us and is
not helpful for our learning!
Some studies show that teenagers’ brains make melatonin, the hormone that
helps sleep, between 11 p.m. and 8 a.m. So it’s better for us to sleep during
these nine hours. But here comes the problem. We’re asked to get to school
by 7:30 a.m. That means we have to get up before 7, when our brains
should still be sleeping. That’s why we’re always so tired and can’t think
clearly in early morning classes. No clear head, no good learning, right?
If we can’t change how our body works, why can’t school start later? In
this way, we can not only sleep more but also learn better!
Want to help us make it happen?
Visit www.nvhsstudsup.org/later-better to learn more.
 brain 大腦 hormone 荷爾蒙

questions.question_text: What problem is Selena Bieber trying to fix?
options: High school lessons are too difficult.
Classes start too early in the morning.
Too many students are late for school.
Lunch break is too short for taking a rest.

source_meta.raw_text:
28. What problem is Selena Bieber trying to fix?
    (A) High school lessons are too difficult.
    (B) Classes start too early in the morning.
    (C) Too many students are late for school.
    (D) Lunch break is too short for taking a rest.

questions.explanation: 根據信件內容，Selena Bieber指出「We're asked to get to school by 7:30 a.m. That means we have to get up before 7, when our brains should still be sleeping」，並提出「If we can't change how our body works, why can't school start later?」，明確表示她要解決的問題是學校開始時間過早，故(B)正確。(A)文中未提及課程難度，(C)信件重點不在遲到問題，(D)完全未涉及午餐時間。

questions.question_text: What can we learn from the letter?
options: Too much melatonin can hurt teenagers’ brain.
The brain stops making melatonin after 11 p.m.
Sleeping longer helps the body make more melatonin.
It is easier to fall asleep when the brain is making melatonin.

source_meta.raw_text:
29. What can we learn from the letter?
    (A) Too much melatonin can hurt teenagers’ brain.
    (B) The brain stops making melatonin after 11 p.m.
    (C) Sleeping longer helps the body make more melatonin.
    (D) It is easier to fall asleep when the brain is making melatonin.

questions.explanation: 根據信件內容，「teenagers' brains make melatonin, the hormone that helps sleep, between 11 p.m. and 8 a.m.」，說明褪黑激素是幫助睡眠的荷爾蒙，當大腦製造褪黑激素時，身體更容易入睡，故(D)正確。(A)曲解原意，信件未提褪黑激素過多會傷害大腦；(B)錯誤，褪黑激素製造時間是從晚上11點到早上8點，不是在11點後停止；(C)無根據，信件未說睡眠時間長短與褪黑激素分泌量的關係。

questions.question_text:
Below is some information from another study.
We studied 48 high school students and found that the students who went
to bed earlier did better on their school tests. This is not only true for
those who slept nine hours, but also true for those who slept less. And
some of them agreed that they learned better in early morning classes.
Do the ideas in Selena Bieber’s letter agree with the information?
options: Yes, because her letter says it is better to sleep from 11 p.m. to 8 a.m.
Yes, because her letter says getting up early is not helpful for learning.
No, because her letter says students are asked to get to school by 7:30 a.m.
No, because her letter says teenagers’ brains do not work well in early morning classes.

source_meta.raw_text:
30. Below is some information from another study.
     We studied 48 high school students and found that the students who went
     to bed earlier did better on their school tests. This is not only true for
     those who slept nine hours, but also true for those who slept less. And
     some of them agreed that they learned better in early morning classes.

    Do the ideas in Selena Bieber’s letter agree with the information?
    (A) Yes, because her letter says it is better to sleep from 11 p.m. to 8 a.m.
    (B) Yes, because her letter says getting up early is not helpful for learning.
    (C) No, because her letter says students are asked to get to school by 7:30 a.m.
    (D) No, because her letter says teenagers’ brains do not work well in early morning classes.

questions.explanation: Selena的信主張「早起對學習沒幫助」，但研究數據顯示「早睡的學生在測驗表現更好，有些人甚至同意在早晨課堂學習更好」，兩者觀點相悖。(D)正確，因為信中聲稱「teenagers' brains do not work well in early morning classes」與研究結果矛盾。(A)錯誤，因為信中提到11 p.m.到8 a.m.是大腦製造褪黑激素的時間，但這不代表同意研究結論；(B)錯誤，信中確實說早起不利學習，但研究卻顯示早睡的學生表現更好，這正是不同意之處；(C)錯誤，7:30 a.m.的上學時間只是陳述事實，不是與研究比較的重點。

media.passage_text:
Kivalina, an island village in Alaska, USA,
is known by only a few people. It cannot be
found on most maps of Alaska because it is
only 10 km2. This small Arctic village is home
to 400 Inuit people*. However, their home
will become uninhabitable because the
island could be covered by the Chukchi
Sea by 2025. These Inuit people will have to leave their home.
But life now is already difficult. Over the past twenty years, winters have
become warmer, the Arctic ice has kept melting, and the sea has been rising.
These changes have made it harder to live by hunting and fishing than before.
What’s worse, there is no Arctic ice thick enough to keep them safe from terrible
wind and rain.
These Inuit people think it is the oil and power companies in Alaska that
have brought all these troubles, but they are the ones who are paying the price.
It would cost hundreds of millions to move their village, and they have no idea
where to get the money. Groups like ReLocate are working together with the
Inuit people to save Kivalina, but nobody is sure if their hard work will come to
anything. All the Inuit people can do now is to pray the rising sea will not cover
their home too soon.
* The Inuit people are a group of people who live in the Arctic.
 melt 融化 hunt 打獵 company 公司

questions.question_text: What can we learn about Kivalina?
options: It is fighting for one last hope.
It has given up its chance to rise.

source_meta.raw_text:
31. What can we learn about Kivalina?
    (A) It is waiting to shine.
    (B) It has become history.
    (C) It is fighting for one last hope.
    (D) It has given up its chance to rise.

questions.explanation: 文章指出Kivalina面臨海平面上升威脅，「their home will become uninhabitable because the island could be covered by the Chukchi Sea by 2025」，但同時提到「Groups like ReLocate are working together with the Inuit people to save Kivalina」，說明雖然處境困難，村民仍在努力爭取最後的希望，故(C)正確。(A)「等待閃耀」過於樂觀，不符合村莊面臨滅頂的現實；(B)「已成歷史」錯誤，村莊仍存在且在抵抗；(D)「放棄上升機會」與文中積極救援的努力相悖。

questions.question_text: What does it mean when we say a place is uninhabitable?

source_meta.raw_text:
32. What does it mean when we say a place is uninhabitable?   
    (A) It is not big.
    (B) It is not popular.
    (C) It cannot be bought.
    (D) It cannot be lived in.

questions.explanation: 題目問 uninhabitable 的意思，文中提到「their home will become uninhabitable because the island could be covered by the Chukchi Sea」，說明家園將無法居住是因為島嶼會被海水淹沒，故 (D) 正確。uninhabitable 由 un-（否定）+ inhabitable（可居住的）組成，直譯為「不可居住的」。(A) 與島嶼大小無關；(B) 與受歡迎程度無關；(C) 與買賣無關，均為錯誤理解。

questions.question_text: What does the writer think about the Inuit people in Kivalina?
options: Their way of living has been hurting the earth.
They might not be able to get enough money to move their village.
They should make the oil and power companies fix their problems.
There are better ways than to move their village to somewhere else.

source_meta.raw_text:
33. What does the writer think about the Inuit people in Kivalina?  
    (A) Their way of living has been hurting the earth.
    (B) They might not be able to get enough money to move their village.
    (C) They should make the oil and power companies fix their problems.
    (D) There are better ways than to move their village to somewhere else.

questions.explanation: 文章明確指出「It would cost hundreds of millions to move their village, and they have no idea where to get the money」，說明因紐特人面臨搬遷村莊需要數億美元但無資金來源的困境，故(B)正確。(A)錯誤，文章未提及因紐特人的生活方式傷害地球，反而指責油氣公司；(C)過度推論，文章雖提及他們認為是油氣公司造成問題，但未建議應由公司負責修復；(D)無根據，文章未討論其他替代方案。

questions.question_text: What does the reading say about the Arctic ice?
options: The Arctic ice covered 10 km2 of Kivalina.
The melting Arctic ice kills about 400 Inuit people each year.
The Arctic ice helped the Inuit people in Kivalina during bad weather.
The melting Arctic ice has given the Inuit people enough water to use.

source_meta.raw_text:
34. What does the reading say about the Arctic ice?      
    (A) The Arctic ice covered 10 km2 of Kivalina.
    (B) The melting Arctic ice kills about 400 Inuit people each year.
    (C) The Arctic ice helped the Inuit people in Kivalina during bad weather.  
    (D) The melting Arctic ice has given the Inuit people enough water to use.

questions.explanation: 文章提到「there is no Arctic ice thick enough to keep them safe from terrible wind and rain」，說明北冰洋冰層原本能在惡劣天氣時保護因紐特人，故(C)正確。(A)混淆了島嶼面積與冰層覆蓋範圍；(B)文中未提及死亡人數；(D)與原意相反，融冰造成海平面上升威脅而非提供水源。

media.passage_text:
After a lot of tests and interviews, you finally got into a good school or got a good
job.  But there’s a problem: it’s a long way from home.  “Well, I can take the bus or
drive,” you might think.  But before you decide whether it’s OK to have such a long
commute, it’s best to know 35 .
Having a long commute means you’ll get fewer hours of sleep, and that’ll make
you feel tired easily.  You’ll also have less time for friends and family, for exercise, or
for a nice meal.
A long commute not only eats up your time but also 36 . Studies show that
people who have a long commute get fat easily and often have neck or back problems.
It’s easier for them to have heart problems too.   37 .  Studies find that these people
get angry more often, feel less happy with their lives, and do less well at work.
Now, does that good school or that good job still sound good to you?
 interview 面試 commute 通勤

questions.question_text: (A) what else you can do
options: how you can enjoy it more
how it will change your life
what is the best way of commute for you

    (B) how you can enjoy it more
    (C) how it will change your life 			
    (D) what is the best way of commute for you

    (C) makes traffic terrible

    (C) And here’s an example
    (D) But that can be stopped

media.passage_text:
NOlympics in Munich
11/11/2013
People in the city of Munich have spoken their mind: they do not
want to host the Winter Olympics in 2022. To the cities that fight for this
chance, 38 . The Olympics usually do a lot of good to the host city. The
Games bring in jobs, visitors, and, most important of all, money. So why
did people in Munich say no?
The Olympics 39 for people in Munich. For them, the world’s
biggest sports festival could invite serious trouble. There would be a lot of
building work before the Games, and during the Games, too much traffic
and too many visitors. Life would become terrible and nature would be
hurt. And 40 : Munich hosted the 1972 Summer Olympics, and its
neighbor, the mountain town Garmisch-Partenkirchen, hosted the 1936
Winter Olympics.
Still, the heavy price Munich paid does not worry the cities that are
in the race to get the 2022 Winter Olympics.         41 , they are happy that
Munich said no to the Olympics—their chances of getting the Games have
become higher.
 host 舉辦 Olympics 奧運 visitor 遊客

questions.question_text: (A) this is surely a surprise

this is perhaps a smart answer
this is old news they have heard

    (D) this is old news they have heard
